name: Stax.RWS
comment:
A Reader Writer State monad stack. This stack cannot fail. Useful for pure computations that need configuration, logging, and mutable state—such as interpreters.

  - The "Reader" part lets you thread configuration through computations. It is like functional dependency injection with the configuration applied LAST.
  - The "Writer" part lets you thread logging through computations. You `tell` to log.
  - The "State" part lets you thread state through computations.

Use RWS when you never need to fail; use `Stax.RWSE` when you need fail-fast error handling.


## Example

Declare small stacks at the top level, then compose them:


    type alias Config =
        { step : Int }

    type alias State =
        { total : Int }

    addStep : RWS Config String State ()
    addStep =
        ask
            |> andThen (\cfg -> modify (\s -> { s | total = s.total + cfg.step }))
            |> andThen_ (tell [ "addStep" ])

    double : RWS Config String State ()
    double =
        modify (\s -> { s | total = s.total * 2 })
            |> andThen_ (tell [ "double" ])

    computation : RWS Config String State ()
    computation =
        addStep
            |> andThen_ double
            |> andThen_ addStep

    -- runWith { step = 1 } { total = 0 } computation
    -- => ( { total = 3 }, [ "addStep", "double", "addStep" ], () )


## Unneeded Facets

Just use unit `()` if you don't need a facet of this. For example,

  - If you declared `type alias MyStack a = RWS () () () a` that would basically do nothing.
  - You could still
      - `get`, `modify`, and `put` the state but it would be unit so uninteresting.
      - `ask` for the configuration but it would be unit so uninteresting.
      - `tell` to log but the result would be a list of units which is uninteresting.


## Functionality By Category


### Type, Construct, Run

@docs RWS, rws, run, runWith, pure, doNothing


### Functor/Applicative/Monad

@docs andMap, andThen, andThen_, map, mapWith, map2, map3, map4, map5, when, whenJust, whenOk


### Foldable/Traversable

@docs combine, combineMap, for_, foldM


### Configuration (Reader)

@docs ask, asks


### Logging (Writer)

@docs tell, andTell, tellSingle


### State

@docs get, gets, modify, put, andModify


### Miscellaneous

@docs askGet


### Transformations

@docs liftStateLens, liftStateOptional, liftStatePrism, ignore

unions.name: RWS
unions.comment:
A Reader Writer State monad stack. This stack cannot fail. Useful for pure computations that need configuration, logging, and mutable state—such as interpreters.

  - The "Reader" part lets you thread configuration through computations. It is like functional dependency injection with the configuration applied LAST.
  - The "Writer" part lets you thread logging through computations. You `tell` to log.
  - The "State" part lets you thread state through computations.

Use RWS when you never need to fail; use `Stax.RWSE` when you need fail-fast error handling.

values.name: andMap
values.comment: Applicative apply. `pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b` lifts a two-argument function over the stack.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state (a -> b) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b

values.name: andModify
values.comment: This convenience function supports modifying the state based on the state, the result of a prior computation, and the configuration.
values.type: (( state, a, config ) -> state) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a

values.name: andTell
values.comment:
This convenience function allows you to log based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state and then return the original value. So it acts like
a kind of sink.
values.type: (( a, config, state ) -> List.List log) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a

values.name: andThen
values.comment: Monadic bind. Chain computations; each step receives the result of the previous.
values.type: (a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b

values.name: andThen_
values.comment:
Chain when the first computation produces `()`. Cleaner than `andThen (\_ -> second)`.

    pure () |> andThen_ (tell [ "Hello" ])
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state () -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b

values.name: ask
values.comment: Read the configuration as the value. See `andModify`, `andTell`, and `mapWith` for alternatives that avoid separate ask/get.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state config

values.name: askGet
values.comment: Want to read the configuration and the state all in one fell swoop? This is it.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ( config, state )

values.name: asks
values.comment: Read a projection of the configuration. Equivalent to `ask |> map f`.
values.type: (config -> a) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a

values.name: combine
values.comment: Run a list of stacks and collect results. Also known as sequence. Equivalent to `combineMap identity`.
values.type: List.List (Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state (List.List a)

values.name: combineMap
values.comment: Map each element through the stack and collect results. Also known as traverse.
values.type: (a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b) -> List.List a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state (List.List b)

values.name: doNothing
values.comment: No-op. Useful with `andThen_` when you need a unit-producing computation.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: foldM
values.comment:
Iterate over a list with an accumulator. Like `List.foldl` but the step runs in the stack (config, state, logging).

    foldM (\acc x -> pure (acc + x)) 0 [ 1, 2, 3 ]
values.type: (localState -> a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state localState) -> localState -> List.List a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state localState

values.name: for_
values.comment:
Like a for loop: run a computation for each element, discarding results.

    for_ [ 1, 2, 3 ] (\n -> tell [ "processing " ++ String.fromInt n ])
values.type: List.List a -> (a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: get
values.comment: Read the current state into the value. Prefer `modify` or `andModify` when you only need to update.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state state

values.name: gets
values.comment: Read a projection of the state. Equivalent to `get |> map f`.
values.type: (state -> a) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a

values.name: ignore
values.comment: Throw away a value.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: liftStateLens
values.type: Internal.Optics.Lens parentState childState -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log childState a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log parentState a

values.name: liftStateOptional
values.comment: Lift a computation to work on a parent state when the child may be missing (Optional/AffineTraversal). The first argument runs if the child is missing; it uses the parent state.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log parentState a -> Internal.Optics.Optional parentState childCaseState -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log childCaseState a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log parentState a

values.name: liftStatePrism
values.comment: Lift a computation to work on a custom type when focusing on one constructor via a Prism. The first argument runs if the constructor does not match.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log customType a -> Internal.Optics.Prism customType constructorInfo -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log constructorInfo a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log customType a

values.name: map
values.comment: Transform the result of a stack.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b

values.name: map2
values.comment: Lift a two-argument function over the stack. Equivalent to `pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b`.
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state c

values.name: map3
values.comment: Lift a three-argument function over the stack.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state c -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state d

values.name: map4
values.comment:
Shorthand for andMapping.

    map4 f a b c d

    -- is equivalent to
    pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b |> andMap c |> andMap d
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state c -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state d -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state e

values.name: map5
values.comment:
Shorthand for andMapping.

    map5 f a b c d e

    -- is equivalent to
    pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b |> andMap c |> andMap d |> andMap e
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state c -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state d -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state e -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state f

values.name: mapWith
values.comment: Map the result of a stack with knowledge of the config and state.
values.type: (( a, config, state ) -> b) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state b

values.name: modify
values.comment: Update the state by applying a function. See `andModify` for a version that also has the prior result and config.
values.type: (state -> state) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: pure
values.comment: Wrap a value in the stack.
values.type: a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a

values.name: put
values.comment: Replace the state entirely.
values.type: state -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: run
values.comment: Execute the stack with config and state. Returns `(finalState, logs, result)`.
values.type: Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> config -> state -> ( state, List.List log, a )

values.name: runWith
values.comment: Same as `run` but takes config and state first. Pipeline-friendly: `runWith config state myStack`.
values.type: config -> state -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a -> ( state, List.List log, a )

values.name: rws
values.comment: Construct a stack from a raw function. Low-level; prefer `pure`, `andThen`, and the other combinators.
values.type: (config -> state -> ( state, List.List log, a )) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state a

values.name: tell
values.comment: Append to the log. All `tell` output is collected and returned at the end of `run`.
values.type: List.List log -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: tellSingle
values.comment: Convenience function to write a single thing.
values.type: log -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: when
values.comment:
Execute a computation only when the condition is true; otherwise do nothing.

    when (count > 0) (modify (\s -> { s | count = s.count - 1 }))
values.type: Basics.Bool -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state () -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: whenJust
values.comment:
Execute a computation only when the Maybe has a value.

    whenJust (Just 42) (\n -> tell [ "got " ++ String.fromInt n ])
values.type: Maybe.Maybe a -> (a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

values.name: whenOk
values.comment:
Execute a computation only when the Result is Ok.

    whenOk (Ok 42) (\n -> tell [ "success: " ++ String.fromInt n ])
values.type: Result.Result e a -> (a -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()) -> Stax.RWS.RWS config log state ()

name: Stax.RWSE
comment:
A Reader Writer State Except monad stack. Useful when pure computations can fail—interpreters, compilers.

  - The "Reader" part lets you thread configuration through computations (e.g., compiler flags). It is like functional dependency injection with the configuration applied LAST.
  - The "Writer" part lets you thread logging through computations. You `tell` to log; messages are read at the end.
  - The "State" part lets you thread state through computations (e.g., variable aliases when unifying, symbols encountered).
  - The "Except" part gives you fail-fast semantics. It is based on `Result` but the API gives you `throw`, `catch`, and `try` which looks like imperative programming.

Use RWSE over RWS when you need fail-fast error handling.


## Example

Declare small stacks at the top level, then compose them. Use `throw` and `catch` when a step can fail:


    type Error
        = ExceededMax Int

    type alias Config =
        { step : Int, maxTotal : Int }

    type alias State =
        { total : Int }

    addStep : RWSE Config String State Error ()
    addStep =
        ask
            |> andThen (\cfg -> modify (\s -> { s | total = s.total + cfg.step }))
            |> andThen_ (tell [ "addStep" ])

    double : RWSE Config String State Error ()
    double =
        modify (\s -> { s | total = s.total * 2 })
            |> andThen_ (tell [ "double" ])

    checkMax : RWSE Config String State Error ()
    checkMax =
        askGet
            |> andThen
                (\( cfg, s ) ->
                    if s.total > cfg.maxTotal then
                        throw (ExceededMax s.total)

                    else
                        tell [ "within limit " ++ String.fromInt s.total ]
                )

    computation : RWSE Config String State Error ()
    computation =
        addStep
            |> andThen_ double
            |> andThen_ checkMax
            |> andThen_ addStep

    -- runWith { step = 1, maxTotal = 5 } { total = 0 } computation
    -- => ( { total = 3 }, [ "addStep", "double", "within limit", "addStep" ], Ok () )


## Unneeded Facets

Just use unit `()` if you don't need a facet of this. For example,

  - If you declared `type alias MyStack a = RWSE () () () () a` that would basically just be `Result`.
  - And you could still
      - `get`, `modify`, and `put` the state but it would be unit so uninteresting.
      - `ask` for the configuration but it would be unit so uninteresting.
      - `tell` to log but the result would be a list of units which is uninteresting.


## Functionality By Category


### Type, Construct, Run

@docs RWSE, rwse, run, runWith, pure, fromResult, doNothing


### Functor/Applicative/Monad

@docs andMap, andThen, andThen_, map, mapWith, map2, map3, map4, map5, when, whenJust, whenOk


### Foldable/Traversable

@docs combine, combineMap, for_, foldM


### Configuration (Reader)

@docs ask, asks


### Logging (Writer)

@docs tell, andTell, tellSingle


### State

@docs get, modify, put, andModify


### Exceptions

@docs catch, throw, try


### Transformations

@docs liftStateLens, liftStateOptional, liftStatePrism, mapError, ignore


### Miscellaneous

@docs askGet

unions.name: RWSE
unions.comment:
A Reader Writer State Except monad stack. Useful when pure computations can fail—interpreters, compilers.

  - The "Reader" part lets you thread configuration through computations (e.g., compiler flags). It is like functional dependency injection with the configuration applied LAST.
  - The "Writer" part lets you thread logging through computations. You `tell` to log; messages are read at the end.
  - The "State" part lets you thread state through computations (e.g., variable aliases when unifying, symbols encountered).
  - The "Except" part gives you fail-fast semantics. It is based on `Result` but the API gives you `throw`, `catch`, and `try` which looks like imperative programming.

Use RWSE over RWS when you need fail-fast error handling.

values.name: andMap
values.comment: Applicative apply. `pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b` lifts a two-argument function over the stack.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error (a -> b) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b

values.name: andModify
values.comment: This convenience function supports modifying the state based on the state, the result of a prior computation, and the configuration.
values.type: (( state, a, config ) -> state) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: andTell
values.comment:
This convenience function allows you to log based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state and then return the original value. So it acts like
a kind of sink.
values.type: (( a, config, state ) -> List.List log) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: andThen
values.comment: Monadic bind. Chain computations; each step receives the result of the previous.
values.type: (a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b

values.name: andThen_
values.comment:
Convenience function for where you have a computation that should happen after another where the preceding produces no value. It reads a lot nicer.

    -- for example
    pure ()
        |> andThen_ (tell [ "Hello" ])

    -- is nicer than
    pure ()
        |> andThen (\_ -> tell [ "Hello" ])
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error () -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b

values.name: ask
values.comment:
Load the configuration as the value.

PLEASE see the following before using this

  - `andModify` if you want to modify the state based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state.
  - `andTell` if you want to log based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state.
  - `mapWith` if you want to map the value with knowledge of the configuration and the current state.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error config

values.name: askGet
values.comment: Convenience function to load the configuration and the state all in one fell swoop.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ( config, state )

values.name: asks
values.comment:
Load one fact of the configuration as the value.

PLEASE see the following before using this

  - `andModify` if you want to modify the state based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state.
  - `andTell` if you want to log based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state.
  - `mapWith` if you want to map the value with knowledge of the configuration and the current state.
values.type: (config -> a) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: catch
values.comment:
Recover from an error. If the stack failed, run the handler with the error; otherwise pass through the success.

    throw (ParseError "bad")
        |> catch (\err -> pure (defaultValue err))
values.type: (error -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: combine
values.comment: ALSO KNOWN AS sequence
values.type: List.List (Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error (List.List a)

values.name: combineMap
values.comment: Map each element through the stack and collect results. Also known as traverse. Fails fast on first error.
values.type: (a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b) -> List.List a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error (List.List b)

values.name: doNothing
values.comment: No-op. Useful with `andThen_` when you need a unit-producing computation.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: foldM
values.comment:
Iterate over a list with an accumulator. Like `List.foldl` but the step runs in the stack (config, state, logging, fail-fast).

    foldM (\acc x -> pure (acc + x)) 0 [ 1, 2, 3 ]
values.type: (localState -> a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error localState) -> localState -> List.List a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error localState

values.name: for_
values.comment:
Like a for loop: run a computation for each element, discarding results. Fails fast on first error.

    for_ [ 1, 2, 3 ] (\n -> tell [ "processing " ++ String.fromInt n ])
values.type: List.List a -> (a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: fromResult
values.comment:
Create a stack from a Result. `Ok` becomes success; `Err` becomes a thrown error.

    fromResult (Ok 42) -- succeeds with 42

    fromResult (Err "fail") -- throws, halting the computation
values.type: Result.Result error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: get
values.comment:
Read the state into the value returned by the state. Only use this if you genuinely need to read the state for some reason.

  - If you want to modify the state (read it, apply a function, and write it) use modify.
  - If you want to modify the state based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state use `andModify`.
  - If you want to tell based on the result of a prior computation, the configuration, and the current state use `andTell`.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error state

values.name: ignore
values.comment: Throw away a value.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: liftStateLens
values.type: Internal.Optics.Lens parentState childState -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log childState error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log parentState error a

values.name: liftStateOptional
values.comment: Lift a computation to work on a parent state when the child may be missing (Optional/AffineTraversal). The first argument runs if the child is missing; it uses the parent state.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log parentState error a -> Internal.Optics.Optional parentState childCaseState -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log childCaseState error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log parentState error a

values.name: liftStatePrism
values.comment: Lift a computation to work on a custom type when focusing on one constructor via a Prism. The first argument runs if the constructor does not match.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log customType error a -> Internal.Optics.Prism customType constructorInfo -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log constructorInfo error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log customType error a

values.name: map
values.comment: Transform the result of a stack.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Shorthand for andMapping.

    map2 Tuple.pair ask get -- RWSE config log state error ( config, state )
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error c

values.name: map3
values.comment:
Shorthand for andMapping.

    map3 f a b c

    -- is equivalent to
    pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b |> andMap c
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error c -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error d

values.name: map4
values.comment:
Shorthand for andMapping.

    map4 f a b c d

    -- is equivalent to
    pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b |> andMap c |> andMap d
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error c -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error d -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error e

values.name: map5
values.comment:
Shorthand for andMapping.

    map5 f a b c d e

    -- is equivalent to
    pure f |> andMap a |> andMap b |> andMap c |> andMap d |> andMap e
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error c -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error d -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error e -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error f

values.name: mapError
values.comment:
Transform the error type. Useful when unifying error types from different subcomputations.

    mapError (\e -> Wrapper e) (parseExpr ...)
values.type: (ea -> eb) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state ea a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state eb a

values.name: mapWith
values.comment: Map the result of a stack with knowledge of the config and state.
values.type: (( a, config, state ) -> b) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error b

values.name: modify
values.comment:
Update the state by applying an update function.

See `andModify` for a more powerful version that includes the current state, the result of a prior computation, and the configuration.
values.type: (state -> state) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: pure
values.comment: Wrap a value in the stack.
values.type: a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: put
values.comment: Replace the state entirely.
values.type: state -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: run
values.comment: Execute the stack with config and state. Returns `(finalState, logs, Result error a)`.
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> config -> state -> ( state, List.List log, Result.Result error a )

values.name: runWith
values.comment: Same as `run` but takes config and state first. Pipeline-friendly: `runWith config state myStack`.
values.type: config -> state -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> ( state, List.List log, Result.Result error a )

values.name: rwse
values.comment: Construct a stack from a raw function. Low-level; prefer `pure`, `andThen`, and the other combinators.
values.type: (config -> state -> ( state, List.List log, Result.Result error a )) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: tell
values.comment: Append to the log. All `tell` output is collected and returned at the end of `run`.
values.type: List.List log -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: tellSingle
values.comment: Append a single value to the log. Equivalent to `tell [ x ]`.
values.type: log -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: throw
values.comment:
Throw an error and halt all computations. Stops chains of `andThen`, `combine`, `combineMap`, `for_`, and `foldM`.

    throw (ParseError "unexpected token")
        |> catch (\err -> pure 0)  -- recovers with 0
values.type: error -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a

values.name: try
values.type: Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error (Result.Result error a)

values.name: when
values.comment:
Execute a computation only when the condition is true; otherwise do nothing.

    when (count > 0) (modify (\s -> { s | count = s.count - 1 }))
values.type: Basics.Bool -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error () -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: whenJust
values.comment:
Execute a computation only when the Maybe has a value.

    whenJust (Just 42) (\n -> tell [ "got " ++ String.fromInt n ])
values.type: Maybe.Maybe a -> (a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()

values.name: whenOk
values.comment:
Execute a computation only when the Result is Ok.

    whenOk (Ok 42) (\n -> tell [ "success: " ++ String.fromInt n ])
values.type: Result.Result e a -> (a -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()) -> Stax.RWSE.RWSE config log state error ()
